name: context-rerank
prompt:
Add a deterministic, code-aware RERANK stage to the Context Compiler.

In agentrail/context/rerank.py expose:
- rerank_candidates(candidates, *, query, top_k) -> dict with keys: method (str), ranked (list), rejected (list), changed (bool)
  - each ranked item carries item['rerank']['signals'], a dict that contains at least the keys symbolOverlap, graphDistance, freshness
  - each rejected item carries item['rerank']['reason'] (a non-empty string)
  - fully deterministic (no model call)

Contract — input candidate shape: rerank_candidates receives a list of candidate dicts (the retrieval output). Each candidate is a dict with these keys: rank (int, 1-based raw retrieval rank), path (str), citation (str), sourceType (str, e.g. 'code'), symbolHints (list[str] of symbol names the candidate mentions/defines), freshness (dict shaped {'status': <str>}, e.g. {'status': 'current'}), reason (str), content (str), and score (dict with at least {'final': float, 'deterministic': float}). The reranker must not mutate the inputs in place beyond attaching the per-item 'rerank' block described above.

Contract — behavior: reorder candidates by deterministic code-aware signals so the most relevant survive a fixed token budget, lifting retrieval precision. method is a non-empty string naming the deterministic method (it must NOT be the pre-rerank lexical method name 'hybrid_lexical_rrf_authority_freshness'). ranked is the kept candidates in best-first order; rejected is the dropped candidates. changed is True exactly when the reranked kept-order differs from the incoming raw retrieval order. A candidate whose symbolHints overlap a symbol named in the query (it DEFINES the queried symbol) must be ranked above a higher-raw-retrieved candidate that only echoes the query keywords with no symbol overlap (e.g. a test/fixture/script file), so ranked[0] is the symbol-defining source in that case. A keyword-noise candidate with no symbol overlap, when a relevant primary source exists, must be moved to rejected with a populated reason. The rerank is deterministic and carries no LLM model (its recorded model is None); the ONLY signals it uses/exposes are the deterministic code-aware ones — set(signals) intersected with {symbolOverlap, graphDistance, freshness} equals exactly {symbolOverlap, graphDistance, freshness}. It keeps equal-or-fewer candidates than the raw top-K — it never pads. With the stage ON, mean precision_at_budget is strictly higher than with it OFF on the existing retrieval fixtures, at equal-or-lower considered-candidate count per fixture and with no individual fixture regressing below its baseline precision.

Wire it into agentrail/context/retrieval.py (query_context) and agentrail/context/packs.py (build_context_pack).
- query_context(repo, query, limit=...) returns a dict with at least keys 'results', 'excluded', and 'compiler'. With rerank ON, for a noisy repo len(results) is strictly LESS than with rerank OFF (it keeps fewer, higher-precision candidates). 'excluded' is a list in which the rerank-rejected candidates appear as dicts each carrying a 'rerank' block; equivalently each rerank-rejected excluded entry is a dict where entry.get('rerank') is truthy.
- The compiler contract must record pack['compiler']['rerank'] (and query_context's out['compiler']['rerank']) as a dict with these exact keys: status (the string 'reranked' when the stage ran), method (the new method name, distinct from 'hybrid_lexical_rrf_authority_freshness'), rankedCandidateIds (camelCase; the non-empty list of ranked candidate ids), rejected (the non-empty-when-applicable list of rejected candidates, each entry a dict carrying a non-empty 'reason' string), model (None), and signals.
- build_context_pack(repo, kind, number, phase) (called as build_context_pack(repo, 'issue', 1, 'plan')) returns a dict with a 'packId'; load_context_pack(repo, packId) returns the pack whose pack['compiler']['rerank'] has status == 'reranked', model is None, and contains 'signals'.
Gate the stage behind the AGENTRAIL_CONTEXT_RERANK environment toggle (value '1' = on, '0' = off) so the baseline (OFF) is measurable.
